title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal risk, but its widespread adoption could lead to potential misuse by attackers to identify unpatched vulnerabilities in systems. Additionally, if the tool contains flaws or is compromised, it could introduce risks to users relying on it.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and security practices before integrating it into workflows.
Monitor for updates or patches to the tool itself to ensure it remains secure.
Educate users on the responsible use of vulnerability scanning tools to prevent misuse.

title: A vulnerability has been found in osuuu LightPicture up to 1.2.2. This issue affects some unknown...
description: A vulnerability has been found in osuuu LightPicture up to 1.2.2. This issue affects some unknown processing of the file /public/install/lp.sql of the component API Upload Endpoint. Such manipulation of the argument key leads to hard-coded credentials. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in osuuu LightPicture up to version 1.2.2 allows remote attackers to exploit hard-coded credentials via the API Upload Endpoint. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, or system compromise if exploited.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to a patched or newer version of osuuu LightPicture, if available.
Restrict access to the vulnerable API endpoint using network-level controls, such as firewalls or IP whitelisting.
Conduct a security audit to identify and mitigate any unauthorized access or data breaches resulting from this vulnerability.

The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in EMQ EMQX Enterprise up to version 6.1.0 allows for improper authorization in the Session Handling component, potentially enabling unauthorized access or privilege escalation. Since the exploit is public and can be launched remotely, it poses a significant risk to systems using this software.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade EMQ EMQX Enterprise to a version newer than 6.1.0, if available. If no patch is available, consider implementing mitigations such as restricting access to the affected component.
Review and monitor logs for any signs of unauthorized access or unusual activity related to session handling to detect potential exploitation attempts.
Implement network-level controls, such as firewalls or VPNs, to restrict remote access to the EMQX service and reduce the attack surface.

title: A weakness has been identified in Wavlink WL-WN579A3 220323. This affects the function sub_401F80...
description: A weakness has been identified in Wavlink WL-WN579A3 220323. This affects the function sub_401F80 of the file /cgi-bin/login.cgi. This manipulation of the argument Hostname causes cross site scripting. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. Upgrading the affected component is recommended. The vendor was contacted early, responded in a very professional manner and quickly released a fixed version of the affected product.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified vulnerability in Wavlink WL-WN579A3 220323 allows for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks via the manipulation of the Hostname argument in the /cgi-bin/login.cgi file. This could enable attackers to inject malicious scripts, potentially compromising user data, session tokens, or executing unauthorized actions on behalf of users.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest fixed version of the Wavlink WL-WN579A3 firmware as provided by the vendor.
Implement web application firewalls (WAFs) to detect and block malicious input targeting XSS vulnerabilities.
Educate users and administrators on recognizing and mitigating the risks of XSS attacks, such as avoiding clicking on suspicious links.

title: The EMC – Easily Embed Calendly Scheduling Features plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored...
description: The EMC – Easily Embed Calendly Scheduling Features plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the plugin's calendly shortcode in all versions up to, and including, 4.4 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user supplied attributes. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

description: The Categories Images plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting in versions up to, and including, 3.3.1, via the 'z_taxonomy_image' shortcode. This is due to the shortcode rendering path passing attacker-controlled class input into a fallback image builder that concatenates HTML attributes without proper escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts that execute when users interact wi

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users with Contributor-level access or higher to inject malicious scripts via the 'z_taxonomy_image' shortcode. recommended_actions: Update the Categories Images plugin to a patched version as soon as a fix is released by the developer.

Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious payloads targeting this vulnerability.

title:
CVE-2026-31413 - In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Fix unsound scalar forking
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Fix unsound scalar forking in maybe_fork_scalars() for BPF_OR

maybe_fork_scalars() is called for both BPF_AND and BPF_OR when the
source operand is a constant.  When dst has signed range [-1, 0], it
forks the verifier state: the pushed path gets dst = 0, the current
path gets dst = -1.

For BPF_AND this is correct: 0 & K == 0.
For BPF_OR this is wrong:    0 | K == K, not 0.

The pushed path therefore tracks dst as 0 when the runtime value is K,
producing an exploitable verifier/runtime divergence that allows
out-of-bounds map access.

Fix this by passing env->insn_idx (instead of env->insn_idx + 1) to
push_stack(), so the pushed path re-executes the ALU instruction with
dst = 0 and naturally computes the correct result for any opcode.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's BPF verifier could allow incorrect state tracking, potentially leading to privilege escalation or bypassing of security mechanisms in systems relying on BPF for sandboxing or filtering. This could expose systems to unauthorized access or data manipulation.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest Linux kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-31413.
Audit and monitor systems using BPF to identify any unusual or unauthorized activity.
Implement additional security measures, such as restricting access to BPF functionality to trusted users or processes, until the patch is applied.

title: CVE-2026-6118 - A vulnerability was determined in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. Impacted is the function add_mcp
description: A vulnerability was determined in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. Impacted is the function add_mcp_server of the file astrbot/dashboard/routes/tools.py of the component MCP Endpoint. ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to perform command injection via the MCP Endpoint, potentially leading to unauthorized system control, data exfiltration, or service disruption.
recommended_actions: Immediately disable or restrict access to the add_mcp_server function until a patch is available.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and implement input validation to mitigate command injection risks.
Contact AstrBotDevs to escalate the issue and request an urgent patch or workaround.

title: CVE-2026-1116 - A Cross-site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability was identified in the `from_dict` method of the `AppLollm
description: A Cross-site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability was identified in the `from_dict` method of the `AppLollmsMessage` class in parisneo/lollms prior to version 2.2.0. The vulnerability arises from the lack of sanitization or HTML encoding of the `content` field when deserializing user-provided data. This allows an attacker to inject malicious HTML or JavaScript payloads, which can be executed in the context of another user's browser. Exploitation of this vulnerability can lead to account takeover, session hijacking, or wormable attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1116.
